result.summary: The LLM API market has consolidated around 5 major providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Meta/open-source, Mistral) with differentiation shifting from raw capability to developer experience, latency, and specialized features like tool use and structured outputs.
key_findings: OpenAI maintains market leadership with GPT-4 Turbo but faces narrowing quality gaps; Claude 3.5 Sonnet leads on coding benchmarks and Gemini 1.5 Pro on long-context tasks
Pricing has compressed 80% in 18 months with GPT-4 class models now available at $3-5/M input tokens, pushing differentiation toward latency and reliability SLAs
Structured outputs (JSON mode, tool calling, function calling) and multimodal input support have become table stakes, with batch processing APIs emerging as the next competitive frontier
